title: Syzygium cagayanense
intro: Syzygium cagayanense, also known as Cagayan water apple or Philippine water apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium cagayanense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Syzygium cagayanense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is a member of the genus Syzygium, which is a group of plants commonly known as "water apples" or "jambus". The Myrtaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 6,000 species. The genus Syzygium is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. Syzygium cagayanense is specifically found in the Philippines.
